maintainer_acceptance.accept_reason: Risk is real-but-unlikely. M3 is self-study homework — the participant follows phase-to-phase without an impulse to edit `source-answer.md` between Phase 1 (generate) and Phase 2 (run separate checker). The 'Do not fix it yet' constraint sits in body prose and that's enough. Moving it into the fence would address a near-zero-probability flow break and add prompt overhead the common case doesn't need.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+)
Exercise quality ladder — target 8, design 9 for peaks
One exercise = one bounded activity
Pattern-recognition LOs need n>=2 instances with contrast
Student agency — adults decide
Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens
Artifact-realism check
Teach the principle and the technique — default
Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go — and forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 body prose (between P1 prompt and Phase 2 header): 'Read the answer quickly. Do not fix it yet.' — 'Do not fix it yet' is a load-bearing forcing function: if the student edits before the checker runs, the contrast design breaks. Speed-runners reading prompt-to-prompt will skip it. It belongs inside the Phase 1 fenced prompt (e.g., appended: 'Do not edit source-answer.md — a separate checker will test it next.').
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Move 'Do not fix it yet' into the Phase 1 fenced prompt as a trailing constraint line. The Phase 1 prompt already ends with a topic drop-in; append one sentence: 'Do not edit the briefing — a separate fact-checker runs next.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Corporate infra is nice-to-have, never a forcing function
Cohort-delivered exercises run on trainer-paced concurrent execution
Host-skill is optional; default cohort is no host-skill
Reflection beats prefer in-chat over file-write
Classroom is the default delivery mode; body teaches the work
Reading burden for verification is banned — Claude audits Claude
Self-report and self-audit charity — verify at artifact, not summary
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 6 prompt closes with 'After saving, tell me: what you fixed / what the final checker still flagged / whether CLAUDE.md needs one sharper rule' — this is a self-summary of what Claude did, with no push-back prompt giving the student a copyable challenge ('which of these did NOT actually change? Be honest.'). The student is left trusting Claude's own account of its fixes.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a paste-ready push-back prompt in Phase 6 body after the fenced prompt: 'Which of the three did NOT actually improve? Be specific — I'd rather know.' Alternatively, instruct the student to open source-answer-final.md and fact-check-final.md directly rather than relying on the chat summary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Offered structures need a forced-engagement mechanic — defaults and plans alike get rubber-stamped
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 3: Claude writes a new CLAUDE.md rule and reports it in chat. No forced-engagement mechanic is included — the student accepts whatever rule Claude authors without being asked to read it critically or rewrite one line that doesn't sound right. The rule goes into the folder as a Claude-authored artifact the student never actively owns.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: After the Phase 3 fenced prompt, add a loose body line (optional push-back for the engaged student): 'Read the rule Claude added. If it would not actually stop you from over-claiming, rewrite it so it would.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: When task selection drives learning outcome, name floor AND ceiling explicitly
Audience tier determines authoring substrate
Plug points + UX — file must be both plug-able and operable
Per-exercise leap test — name three observable Monday-morning outcomes
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block has no dedicated leap-test block naming three observable verbs the student exhibits on their own work by next working day. The Meta section describes the method but states no falsifiable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a 'Leap test' block in the maintainer section with three observable bullets, e.g.: 'runs a separate fact-checker subagent on a Claude-generated briefing before sending it'; 'adds a source-verification rule to CLAUDE.md in their working folder'; 'compares two fact-check rounds and identifies which claims improved and which didn't.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function names its dominant failure and one move that recovers
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block contains no per-phase failure-mode or escape-hatch rows. Phases 2, 5, and 6 each invoke a subagent fact-checker; dominant failure (subagent re-uses the generator's session context and rubber-stamps the answer rather than checking independently) is not named and no escape hatch (e.g., 'if findings look identical to the answer, run the checker in a fresh Cowork session') is documented.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a failure-modes section to the maintainer block covering at minimum Phase 2/5/6: dominant failure = checker inherits generator context and self-validates; escape hatch = open a genuinely new Cowork session for the checker, or paste source-answer.md text cold into the checker prompt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Heavy fan-out prompts get a body-side recovery callout
Convenience-driven prompts with known shape-bias get body-side acknowledgment callouts
Deliberate-antipattern prompts are class-judge exempt — flagged-by-design
Session widget — source shape; place by learning flow
Pre-action framing blocks don't preview the prompt's payoff
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 5 body between session widget and fenced prompt: 'Watch whether Cowork actually runs this as a separate checker. The point is not the label; the point is that the checker reads the sources and the answer as its own job, then reports findings instead of rewriting.' This previews the felt-evidence the Phase 5 prompt is meant to surface — the student arrives knowing what to look for rather than discovering it.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Cut the 'Watch whether... rewriting.' sentences from Phase 5 body. The observation can move to a post-prompt body line (past-tense recap shape) after the student has run the prompt and seen the result.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Multi-session ships as packaging, not as a new taught primitive
/clear is a tool with a purpose, not a ceremony
